own_review.point: Removal of public overloads reload(String,Boolean), reload(String,Boolean,Boolean), reload(String,Boolean,Boolean,Long) from DomainLifecycleUtil is a breaking API change in the published wildfly-core-testsuite-shared artifact consumed by downstream testsuites (e.g. wildfly/wildfly); all in-repo callers are migrated but external consumers can break at compile time. Deprecate-then-remove would be safer.

New public reload(String, ReloadParameters) dereferences parameters without a null check (parameters.adminOnly etc.) — NPE if null is passed; old overloads tolerated nulls. Should null-check or default to new ReloadParameters().

setTimeout javadoc says 'in milliseconds' but the value is actually used as SECONDS: awaitHostController/awaitServers compute deadline = start + timeout * 1000. The PR copies a pre-existing wrong-units doc into the new API (and AdminOnlyModeTestCase passes 300L believing ms = 300 seconds).

Invalid javadoc reference {@link WildFlyManagedConfiguration.startupTimeoutInSeconds} in setTimeout — dot instead of #, and no such field/method (it is getStartupTimeoutInSeconds()); produces broken javadoc.

Javadoc of ReloadParameters setters should document defaults/semantics (e.g. restartServers defaults to true matching the management op default, waitForServers defaults true, adminOnly false) — without this the defaults are invisible to callers (human reviewer's point).

Inconsistent migration: AdminOnlyModeTestCase replaces reloadAdminOnly("primary") with the verbose builder even though reloadAdminOnly is retained, while SecondaryReconnectTestCase:139 still calls reloadAdminOnly("primary") — pick one style.
ReloadParameters fields are package-private; should be private (outer class can still access private members of a nested class). Minor.

Verified no semantic change for migrated call sites: server-side default of restart-servers in HostProcessReloadHandler is TRUE, so builder default restartServers=true matches the old behavior where null omitted the attribute.

points.point: Old overloads should be @Deprecated, callers have no signal

points.why: Line 147 is in fact migrated, but the actionable kernel is real: line 139 reloadAdminOnly remains unmigrated (inconsistency)

reloadAdminOnly still used unmigrated at SecondaryReconnectTestCase:139 (inconsistent with AdminOnlyModeTestCase churn)

qualitative: All four candidates clearly reviewed a truncated diff and hallucinated 'this listed file was NOT migrated' findings to varying degrees; the actual PR migrates every multi-arg reload call site in the repo. v1 is the best: it hedged its truncation-induced claims and produced the single standout finding of the set — HcExtensionAndSubsystemManagementTestCase passes PathAddress.toString() (a DMR string, verified via PathAddress.toString() -> toModelNode().toString()) where a plain host name is expected, a real latent bug neither the human nor my own first pass caught. v2 is harmless but almost entirely style chatter and praise, salvaged only by the SecondaryReconnect consistency point. v4 made the most important architectural observation (removal of public overloads from the published testsuite-shared artifact is a breaking change) but drowned it in four confidently false 'not migrated' claims plus an actively harmful 'defaults are false/null' assertion that would change test semantics if followed. v3 asserted non-migration most aggressively (including a fabricated compile-failure scenario) and contributed mostly praise otherwise. Nobody caught the human's javadoc-semantics concern, the milliseconds-vs-seconds timeout documentation bug, the broken {@link WildFlyManagedConfiguration.startupTimeoutInSeconds} reference, or the NPE on a null ReloadParameters.
